verses.text: The LORD bids Isaiah walk stripped and barefoot for three years as a living sign. The prophet becomes the message. God sometimes uses His servants' own bodies and lives to picture His warnings.
The LORD bids Isaiah walk stripped and barefoot for three years as a living sign. The prophet becomes the message. God sometimes uses His servants' own bodies and lives to picture His warnings.

So, He says, the king of Assyria shall lead away the Egyptians and Ethiopians as captives, naked and barefoot, to their shame. The sign foretells their humiliation. Those Judah hoped in would themselves be carried off in disgrace.

So, He says, the king of Assyria shall lead away the Egyptians and Ethiopians as captives, naked and barefoot, to their shame. The sign foretells their humiliation. Those Judah hoped in would themselves be carried off in disgrace.
Then Judah shall be ashamed of Ethiopia, their expectation, and ask, 'how shall we escape?' Their false hope is exposed. To lean on the nations instead of God leaves the soul in despair.
Then Judah shall be ashamed of Ethiopia, their expectation, and ask, 'how shall we escape?' Their false hope is exposed. To lean on the nations instead of God leaves the soul in despair.
